problems.title: Do robust invariants over goal self-modification actually exist for sufficiently
problems.summary: Do robust invariants over goal self-modification actually exist for sufficiently capable systems under computational irreducibility, and if so what forms can they take? The conjecture relies on the id
problems.full_text: Do robust invariants over goal self-modification actually exist for sufficiently capable systems under computational irreducibility, and if so what forms can they take? The conjecture relies on the idea that safety should target the minimal generative rule and preserve stable constraints there, but it is unresolved whether meaningful invariants are expressible, enforceable, or preserved under self-interpretation and self-rewrite. This is a central open question because if such invariants are impossible or too weak, the practical safety program implied by the conjecture may collapse.

problems.title: How can a system's meta-criteria for goal revision be made truth-tracking, non-g
problems.summary: How can a system's meta-criteria for goal revision be made truth-tracking, non-gameable, and stably corrigible without merely relocating the alignment problem one level up? The conjecture says meaning
problems.full_text: How can a system's meta-criteria for goal revision be made truth-tracking, non-gameable, and stably corrigible without merely relocating the alignment problem one level up? The conjecture says meaningful self-revision requires asymmetry and some more stable evaluative structure, but this raises an unresolved regress: what validates the validator, and under what conditions does the meta-level avoid Goodharting, self-sealing coherence, or adversarial exploitation?

problems.title: Under what conditions does goal-reflection actually improve safety rather than c
problems.summary: Under what conditions does goal-reflection actually improve safety rather than create a new attack surface or instability? The conjecture treats reflective revision as load-bearing, but self-modificat
problems.full_text: Under what conditions does goal-reflection actually improve safety rather than create a new attack surface or instability? The conjecture treats reflective revision as load-bearing, but self-modification of ends may drift, Goodhart on flawed meta-criteria, become manipulable by adversaries, or erase human-desired constraints. The unresolved tension is whether corrigibility about ends is robustly achievable, or whether reflective goal revision often just relocates the alignment problem to a more dangerous meta-level.
